name: Jill's Focaccia
recipeIngredient: ½ cup pine nuts (toasted or raw)
recipeInstructions:
1. Make a slack dough by hand, electric mixer, or bread machine with the first 9 ingredients.
2. Add the olives and pine nuts just before the last ½ cup flour if kneading by hand or using a mixer, or 5 minutes before the end of the bread machine cycle (to keep them whole).
3. Let dough double in size about 1 ½ hours.
4. Punch down and spread in a cornmeal-dusted, heavily dusted 14-inch round pizza pan.
5. Make indentations in the dough with your fingertips.
6. Cover and let rise for 45 minutes or until doubled and puffy.
7. Push vegetables of choice into the dough to keep them anchored down.
8. Preheat oven to 500°F (250°C). Reduce temperature to 450°F (230°C).
9. Bake foccacia for 15-20 minutes, or until golden brown.
10. Remove from oven and sprinkle with fresh basil and cracked black pepper. Fresh tomato slices, chopped garlic, and a bit of olive oil elevate this bread to sublime.
Note: Italian flour has the lowest level of ash, protein at 9.8%, and is used to add lightness to pizza and other baked goods. Diastatic malt powder (or barley malt syrup) helps produce a finer texture and longer keeping quality when added to dough.
